runs.answer:
⟦panel-data⟧
{
  "Voices": {
    "type": "item-card",
    "title": "Voices",
    "payload": {
      "name": "Voices",
      "base": "Large Cluster Jewel",
      "rarity": "unique",
      "slot": "jewel",
      "mods": [
        { "kind": "explicit", "text": "Adds 3 Jewel Socket Passive Skills" },
        { "kind": "explicit", "text": "<1, 3, 5, or 7> Small Passive Skills which grant nothing" },
        { "kind": "implicit", "text": "Corrupted" }
      ]
    }
  }
}
⟦/panel-data⟧

Voices ⟦panel*:item-card:Voices⟧ is a unique Large Cluster Jewel that strips the passive tree down to pure jewel-socket real estate, exile. Its text is simple: **Adds 3 Jewel Socket Passive Skills** and **Adds <1, 3, 5, or 7> Small Passive Skills which grant nothing**. Those small passives are empty nodes — no stats, no effect — pure travel tax between you and the three jewel sockets. The jewel itself comes **corrupted** by default.

The mechanic is unusual. Most cluster jewels add notables and beneficial small passives; Voices inverts that — you socket it into a Large Cluster Jewel slot on your outer passive tree and all you get are three fresh jewel sockets at the cost of 4–8 passive points (depending on variant) to path through the empty nodes. You then socket *other* cluster jewels (typically Medium or Small) into those three sockets, and those nested jewels bring the actual notables and stats you want. Voices is the **jewel-slot multiplier** — it lets you allocate up to three more cluster jewels in a single Large socket, each with their own notables and jewel sockets if Medium-size.

The four variants differ only in how many empty small passives you must allocate to reach all three jewel sockets: **1-passive** (4 points total to reach all three sockets), **3-passive** (5 points), **5-passive** (7 points), **7-passive** (8 points). The 1-passive version is the chase variant with a spawn weight of only 5 (versus 670 for the 7-passive). The fewer the travel nodes, the more efficient the jewel-socket density — a 1-passive Voices is the most point-efficient way to stack cluster jewel notables in PoE1.

**Acquisition is drop-restricted.** Voices cannot be chanced. It drops exclusively from `Simulacrum` encounters (the Delirium endgame encounter opened via Simulacrum fragments), specifically after completing a wave that spawns the bosses `Kosis, The Revelation` or `Omniphobia, Fear Manifest`. Version 3.25.0 added more opportunities to obtain it by defeating those bosses mid-wave. You can also obtain one via the divination card `Luminous Trove` (7-card set that rewards a corrupted Voices), or from `The Void` (random div-card set exchange).

**Why use it?** Voices enables **jewel-stacking** builds — builds that derive most of their power from stacking many copies of a specific cluster jewel notable (e.g. `Heraldry` for herald effect, `Pure Aptitude` for attributes, `Vengeful Commander` for aura effect). A single Voices socketed on the outer tree lets you attach three Medium Cluster Jewels, each of which can host two notables plus two more Small Cluster Jewel sockets (6 more jewels if you nest Smalls). The math compounds: one Voices → 3 Mediums → 6 Smalls = 9 cluster jewels from one Large socket. Builds that need many copies of a notable (or many jewel sockets for `Split Personality` scaling) rely on this structure.

**Trade-offs.** You lose all the notables and stats the original Large Cluster Jewel would have granted. A normal Large Cluster Jewel gives 2–3 notables plus 8–12 small passives with stats; Voices gives 0 notables and 0 stats from the jewel itself — only sockets. You must fill those sockets with strong nested cluster jewels or the point investment is wasted. The jewel is also **corrupted**, so you cannot modify it further. Finally, it's expensive — 1-passive Voices is one of the most expensive jewels in any temp league because of the 5-weight rarity and the Simulacrum-boss gate.

**Path.** Farm Simulacrum encounters (requires 300 `Simulacrum Splinters` to assemble one Simulacrum map device use, obtained from Delirium content). Push waves 18–20 (the boss waves) and pray for the drop. Alternatively, buy one on trade — expect 30–200 divines for a 1-passive, 5–20 divines for a 3-passive, 1–5 divines for a 5-passive or 7-passive depending on league economy. Check the listed passive count carefully; the variants look identical until you read the mod line.

Synergies:

- **Megalomaniac** — Medium Cluster Jewel that grants 3 random cluster notables with only 4 small passives that grant nothing. Socket this into one of Voices' three jewel sockets to get 3 notables for 5 points. Pairs naturally because Megalomaniac also strips small-passive stats in favor of notable density.

- **Split Personality** — Crimson Jewel that scales its two random modifiers (`+5 to <Stat>`, `+40 to <Defence>`) by `+25%` per allocated passive between the jewel socket and your class starting location. Voices jewel sockets are far from the tree centre, so Split Personality socketed into a Voices-hosted Medium or Small cluster jewel can grant huge attribute or defence stacking (40+ travel points × 25% = 10× multiplier on base stats). Limited to 2 Split Personalities per build (version 3.11.0).

- **Herald-stacking builds** — historically used 3–6 copies of Voices to stack `Heraldry`, `Endbringer`, and `Purposeful Harbinger` notables on nested Medium Cluster Jewels. Purposeful Harbinger was nerfed heavily in 3.11.0 but the jewel-stacking archetype survives in other forms (aura-stacking, attribute-stacking, specific-notable abuse).

- **Efficient jewel-socket pathing** — any build that values jewel sockets more than the notables on the outer tree (e.g. Siege Ballista, Explosive Arrow totems, Spectres, or any build with many `Eminence` / `Might` / `Sacrificial Zeal` threshold jewels) can use a 1-passive Voices to fit more jewels without traveling the outer ring.

Sources:
- [Wiki: Voices](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Voices)
- [Wiki: Megalomaniac](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Megalomaniac)
- [Wiki: Split Personality](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Split_Personality)
- [Wiki: Simulacrum](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Simulacrum)
- [Wiki: Cluster jewel](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Cluster_jewel)Voices unlocks two dominant archetypes, exile: **aura-stackers** and **attribute-stackers**. Both lean on jewel-socket density to concentrate many copies of a scarce-but-potent notable or jewel type.

## 1. Aura-stacking (Guardian / Scion)

Aura-stacking builds reserve 8–12 auras simultaneously and scale their **aura effect** through cluster jewel notables until every aura grants 2–3× its base value. The build's power comes not from a single skill but from the sum of all aura multipliers applied to one elemental skill (often Spark, Blade Vortex, or minions). Voices is the scaffold: socket 2–3 copies into Large Cluster Jewel slots on the outer tree, fill their Medium jewel sockets with `Heraldry` / `Endbringer` / `Purposeful Harbinger` notables (aura-effect cluster jewels), and the math compounds — a Guardian with three Voices can allocate 9 Medium Cluster Jewels (18 notables) for 20–30 passive points.

**Purposeful Harbinger** was the engine notable pre-nerf: **Auras from your Skills have 8% increased Effect on you for each Herald affecting you, up to 40%**. Version 3.11.0 added the 40% cap *globally* across all copies of the notable, so now players use 2–3 Purposeful Harbinger notables paired with 2–3 Heralds to reach the cap. The archetype survived the nerf by shifting to other aura-effect sources (`Heraldry`, `Endbringer`, ascendancy nodes, rare jewels with `1% increased Aura Effect` corruption) — a modern aura-stacker with three 1-passive Voices can still push Wrath / Anger / Determination / Grace / Discipline / Precision / Purity of Elements / Smite / Zealotry / Clarity / Herald of Ice / Herald of Thunder to 150–200% effect, granting 10,000+ ES and millions of DPS.

The ascendancy choice is `Guardian` (Radiant Crusade + Unwavering Crusade + Time of Need) or `Scion` (Guardian + something else). Guardian provides base aura effect + flat ES per aura, which scales exponentially with the jewel-stacking plan. Trade-off: you reserve 95–99% of mana, so life sustain comes from ES + Energy Shield on Hit Watcher's Eye or Discipline leech.

## 2. Attribute-stacking (Strength / Intelligence / Dexterity)

Attribute-stacking builds convert a single attribute (usually `Strength` or `Intelligence`) into offensive and defensive stats via synergy uniques and notables, then use Voices to socket many copies of `Split Personality` and `Pure Aptitude` (or `Pure Might` / `Pure Guile`) cluster jewels to amplify that attribute to 2,000–3,000+.

`Split Personality` is the core jewel: **This Jewel's Socket has 25% increased effect per Allocated Passive Skill between it and your Class' starting location**. The base jewel grants two random modifiers (e.g. `+5 to Strength`, `+40 to Armour`). Socket a Split Personality into a Voices-hosted Medium or Small cluster jewel on the outer tree — 40+ travel nodes from the centre — and the `+5 to Strength` becomes `+5 × (1 + 0.25 × 40) = +55 to Strength` per jewel. Stack two Split Personalities (the limit since 3.11.0) in far-flung Voices sockets and you gain +110–220 to your chosen attribute before any other gear. Pair with `Brutus' Lead Sprinkler` (fire damage per 5 Strength), `Howa` (lightning attack damage per 10 Int), `Pillar of the Caged God` (attack damage per 10 Str), or `Whispering Ice` (Int-stacking cold DoT caster), and the jewel's placement becomes a damage multiplier.

`Pure Aptitude` (Medium cluster notable) grants **+25 to all Attributes** for 5 passive points when socketed in a Voices-hosted Medium. Socket three Mediums with Pure Aptitude into one Voices and you gain +75 to all Attributes for 12 points — density that the outer tree cannot match. Attribute-stackers use this to meet attribute requirements for high-Int gems (Awakened, level 21+) while funneling every other slot toward one attribute.

The path: farm or buy 1–3× 1-passive Voices (30–200 divines each in a temp league), buy 2× Split Personality with the correct attribute rolls (10–50 divines each for Strength or Intelligence), craft or buy 6–9 Medium Cluster Jewels with `Pure Aptitude` or aura-effect notables, and socket them in a cascading tree extending from the outer Large Cluster Jewel sockets. Final tree shape: 3–5 Large sockets occupied by Voices → 9–15 Medium Cluster Jewels → up to 30 Small Cluster Jewels if you nest further. Point investment: 40–80 passives for the jewel structure alone.

Trade-offs are steep. You lose all baseline defensive nodes from the regular tree — resistance, life, armour, evasion — because most passives are allocated in the jewel web. Aura-stackers offset this with huge ES pools (`10,000+ ES`) from aura-effect scaling on Discipline; attribute-stackers offset with `1,500–2,500 life` plus armour / evasion from Strength / Dexterity scaling or `8,000+ ES` from Intelligence. Both archetypes are **late-game** — the jewels are drop-restricted to Simulacrum (Voices, Split Personality) and expensive; you cannot league-start this. Budget floor: 100–500 divines for a functional version, 1,000+ divines for a min-maxed 1-passive Voices variant.

Synergies:

- **Megalomaniac** — Medium Cluster Jewel that grants 3 random notable passives with 4 small passives that grant nothing. Socket into a Voices jewel socket to gain 3 notables for 5 passive points. If it rolls Purposeful Harbinger + Heraldry + Endbringer, a single Megalomaniac can replace three separate Medium Cluster Jewels in an aura-stacker.

- **Heraldry** (Medium cluster notable) — **Auras from your Skills have 3% increased Effect on you** per small passive on the cluster jewel (typically 12% total). Stack 6–9 copies via Voices to amplify every aura.

- **Endbringer** (Medium cluster notable) — **20% increased Effect of Herald Buffs on you**. Heralds grant flat damage, so doubling Herald of Ice / Thunder / Ash via Endbringer + Purposeful Harbinger turns them into primary damage sources.

- **Pure Aptitude** (Medium cluster notable) — **+25 to all Attributes**. The most efficient attribute source per passive point when socketed in a Voices-hosted Medium.

- **Aul's Uprising** (amulet with "Reserves no Mana" for one aura) — frees 25–50% mana reservation so aura-stackers can fit one more aura without investing in Reservation Efficiency.

- **Prism Guardian** (shield, "Socketed Gems Reserve Life instead of Mana") — lets aura-stackers reserve 3 auras on life instead of mana, freeing mana for more auras or Eldritch Battery builds.

Sources:
- [Wiki: Voices](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Voices)
- [Wiki: Cluster jewel](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Cluster_jewel)
- [Wiki: Purposeful Harbinger](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Purposeful_Harbinger)
- [Wiki: Split Personality](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Split_Personality)
- [Wiki: Megalomaniac](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Megalomaniac)

tools.output_excerpt: {"plain_text":"Several cluster jewels in progression Cluster jewels are a type of jewels that can be socketed in the outermost sockets of the passive skill tree (known as Large Jewel Sockets ). These jewels extend the skill tree by creating new clusters of nodes, which can contain small nodes which can have their stats modified by the jewel, notable passives, and even keystones . They can also create more jewel sockets (Medium or Small Jewel Sockets) which can support smaller cluster jewels. They are occasionally dropped from Delirium encounters or rewards. Cluster jewels can be crafted to modify their properties. Like all jewels, rare cluster jewels can have a maximum of four affixes, and can receive corrupted implicit modifiers from the base jewel pool. Contents 1 Mechanics 2 Optimal passive skill amounts 3 List of cluster jewels 4 List of unique cluster jewels 5 Jewel modifiers by jewel type 6 List of keystone nodes 7 List of notable passives 8 List of minor passives 9 Version history Mechanics edit Non-unique Cluster Jewels come with two enchantments : one that determines how many passive skills are in the cluster, and one that determines what bonuses the passives in the jewel give. The latter determines what kind of mods the Cluster Jewel can roll. Like other jewels, they can have up to four explicit modifiers. The explicit mods will either modify or add bonuses to the small passives or add a notable skill to the cluster. Unlike other jewels, normal rarity Cluster Jewels can drop. Medium and Large Cluster Jewels have a third enchantment that determines how many jewel sockets it has. Jewel sockets in the cluster can support either regular or Abyss jewels or smaller-sized cluster jewels. Medium Jewel Sockets in a Large Cluster Jewel Large Cluster Jewel Place into an allocated Large Jewel Socket on the Passive Skill Tree. Added passives do not interact with jewel radiuses. Right click to remove from the Socket. can support Medium Medium Cluster Jewel Place into an allocated Medium or Large Jewel Socket on the Passive Skill Tree. Added passives do not interact with jewel radiuses. Right click to remove from the Socket. or Small Cluster Jewels Small Cluster Jewel Place into an allocated Small, Medium or Large Jewel Socket on the Passive Skill Tree. Added passives do not interact with jewel radiuses. Right click to remove from the Socket. , and Small Jewel Sockets in a Medium Cluster Jewel Medium Cluster Jewel Place into an allocated Medium or Large Jewel Socket on the Passive Skill Tree. Added passives do not interact with jewel radiuses. Right click to remove from the Socket. can support a Small Cluster Jewel Small Cluster Jewel Place into an allocated Small, Medium or Large Jewel Socket on the Passive Skill Tree. Added passives do not interact with jewel radiuses. Right click to remove from the Socket. . You can have a maximum of two sockets for Large and one for Medium. Passives from the Cluster Jewel are never considered to be within radius of Jewels which interact with nearby passives. Similarly, jewels with a radius have radius effects disabled if they're socketed in a Cluster Jewel jewel socket. The passive points spent on the Cluster Jewel passives can be refunded by simply unsocketing the jewel, which refunds all passive points spent on that jewel's cluster. However, if a jewel socket or Keystone passive has been allocated, it will cost 1 refund point to unsocket it. No jewel will have more than one of a given notable, but duplicates of the same notable skills can be stacked. If the notable has a limit to its effect, such as Purposeful Harbinger , that limit applies across all instances of the notable. Selling 5 of the same kind(e.g. Large and Rare ) will give you a new jewel of the same size and rarity . The new jewel will have the minimum item level from the 5 components, a new enchantment and new modifiers . Small Cluster Jewel Small Cluster Jewel Place into an allocated Small, Medium or Large Jewel Socket on the Passive Skill Tree. Added passives do not interact with jewel radiuses. runs.answer:
Your weakest defensive layer is **physical mitigation**, exile — you face the physical world almost naked.

At `phys:25%` damage reduction and a physical max-hit of only **46,190**, you can be one-shot by any rare mob with a double-attack or Sirus's corridor-beam (which has a substantial physical component). Compare that to your elemental max-hits: `fire:164,483`, `cold:164,483`, `lit:164,483` — those are **3.5× higher** than physical. Against an endgame physical hit you are tissue.

You run a pure mana-stacker archetype (`life:3,676` / `mana:9,636` / `ES:3,692` = 17,004 combined pool). You have `5` endurance charges current and max, which gives you `20%` physical reduction baseline before armour — already accounted for in your `25%` total. But you sit at only **416 armour**, which is essentially zero against large hits. Armour's effectiveness formula is `armour / (armour + 10 × raw_hit)` — when a Shaper slam lands for 8,000 raw physical, your 416 armour contributes `416 / (416 + 80,000) = 0.5%` mitigation on top of the endurance-charge base. You're relying entirely on your large combined pool to absorb hits, with no meaningful reduction layer.

Your notes acknowledge this: "Combining our life, mana and energy shield we actually have a huge pool…combined with Endurance charges this means we can tank things like Shaper slams." That's true for *some* slams when Arcane Cloak is up — but Arcane Cloak has a cooldown and you cannot keep it running during trash-dense juiced maps or multi-phase boss fights. Between Cloak windows, a single physical crit from a Juggernaut rare in a red map will drop you.

Three ways to shore this up:

1. **Granite Flask of the Armadillo** — swap one of your utility flasks (the second Jade or one Quicksilver) for a Granite with the `% increased Armour` suffix. With your `Flagellant's` prefix you already have charge-on-hit sustain; the Granite gives `+3,000` base armour during flask effect, pushing your sheet armour to ~3,400 in combat. That lifts your physical max-hit from 46k to ~80k — still not stellar but survivable in reds.

2. **Armor / Evasion base on chest** — your `Sadist Garb` (pure Evasion/ES base) gives you zero armour contribution. Swap to an `Assassin's Garb` (Evasion/ES/Armour hybrid) or a `Zodiac Leather` (pure EV/AR) with the same life + mana + suppress rolls. You lose nothing offensively and gain 400–600 base armour from the implicit alone, which scales with your tree's `% increased defences from equipped shield` and `% increased armour and evasion` nodes.

3. **Enduring Composure small cluster** — allocate the `Enduring Composure` small-cluster passive (or craft it on a jewel): **"Gain an Endurance Charge every second if you've been Hit Recently."** This keeps your 5 endurance charges topped up even against DoT-only bosses (Maven memory game, Searing Exarch ground burn) where you don't get hit frequently enough to proc your current on-kill charge sustain. Endurance charges are your only reliable phys mitigation; keeping them up 100% of the time is non-negotiable.

Your `chaos:71%` is excellent (well over the functional cap for non-Divine Flesh builds). Your `100%` spell suppression is capped. Your elemental resistances are capped. Your combined pool is massive. But all of that is bypassed the moment a Metamorph winds up a physical slam — you have no layer to absorb it, only the pool to tank it raw. Fix physical first.

**Sources:**
- [Wiki: Armour](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Armour)
- [Wiki: Endurance Charge](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Endurance_charge)
- [Wiki: Enduring Composure](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Enduring_Composure)**Slot: Belt. Target mod: `+#% to all Elemental Resistances` prefix. Cost: 10–30 chaos.**

Your belt is a `Stygian Vise` with `+110` life, `+73` mana, `chaos:20%`, and a crafted dual-resist suffix. It gives you **zero armour** — Stygian Vise is a leather base with no inherent defensive stat beyond the life roll. Swap it for a **Granite Flask with the `Flagellant's` prefix** (gear-only fix means flask counts) — it costs 5–15 chaos for a white base, then 3 alchs to roll. That's 10–30 chaos total, well under any gear alternative.

A `Flagellant's Granite Flask` with a defensive suffix (Armour, Stun avoidance, or `of Iron Skin` for +50% armour during effect) adds **+3,000 base armour** during flask effect. Your current `20%` block means you're being hit every 4 swings on average — `Flagellant's` triggers on every hit (3 charges per hit), so uptime in combat is 95%+. That 3,000 armour scales with your existing `% increased armour` on tree (you have a few Armour/ES hybrid nodes from Hierophant pathing), pushing your in-combat armour from 416 to ~3,400. Against an 8,000 raw phys slam (Shaper tier), that lifts mitigation from `0.5%` (your current 416) to **~30%** (3,400 armour), raising your physical max-hit from `phys:46,190` to ~66,000 — survivable with your 17k combined pool.

Your current flask belt is already 5× `Flagellant's` prefix magic flasks. Slot 1 is a life flask (not replaceable), slots 2–5 are Jade/Quicksilver/Silver/Quartz utility flasks. Drop the second Jade (flask 36, lowest priority for a caster) and add the Granite. You lose 1,500 evasion during one flask window; you gain the ability to not die to Physical Spell Cascade on Sirus.

**Trade search parameters** (Settlers league):
- Flask type: `Granite Flask`.
- Prefix: `Flagellant's` (`Gain 3 Charges when you are Hit by an Enemy`).
- Suffix: `of the Armadillo` (`#% increased Armour during Flask effect`) or `of Iron Skin` (`+#% to Armour during Flask effect`).
- Quality: 20% (optional — 4 Glassblower's Baubles to max it yourself).

No chest swap required — your `Sadist Garb` (pure Evasion/ES base) has no armour implicit and cannot be rerolled for one without losing your Exarch/Eater implicits. The granite flask is instant, tradeable, and the cheapest armour injection in the game.

**Sources:**
- [Wiki: Granite Flask](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Granite_Flask)
- [Wiki: Flask Suffix List](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/List_of_flask_suffix_mods)
- [Wiki: Armour](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Armour)I need to clarify your question, exile — which **mod** are you asking about? Your question follows two separate recommendations:

1. **The gear fix I just proposed** — a `Flagellant's Granite Flask` (flask base, not gear).
2. **The belt swap I mistakenly mentioned first** — a `Stygian Vise` replacement with `+#% to all Elemental Resistances` (which was an error — that doesn't fix your physical mitigation at all).

If you're asking about the **flask**, the answer is:

- **Base:** `Granite Flask` (any variant — Granite Flask, Greater Granite Flask, Grand Granite Flask).
- **ilvl:** `1` minimum. Flask prefixes and suffixes have no item-level requirement — you can roll `Flagellant's` and any armour suffix on an ilvl 1 white Granite Flask.

If you're asking about a **gear piece that grants armour natively and can be crafted with defensive mods**, the answer is:

- **Base:** `Crystal Belt` (the only belt base with an armour implicit — but that implicit is `+#% to all Elemental Resistances`, **not** armour). Belts in PoE1 do not have armour implicits. The only way to get armour on a belt is via explicit suffix mods like `+# to Armour`.
- **ilvl for `+# to Armour` suffix:** `ilvl 1` (lowest tier, `+41–80`), up to `ilvl 84` for the highest tier (`+386–430`).

But a belt with `+400 armour` explicit costs 50–100 chaos for a rare with life + mana + resists, and only contributes ~400 armour total — far weaker than the Granite Flask's 3,000.

**The cheapest gear-only fix remains the Granite Flask.** Were you asking about a different mod, or did you want the chest-base alternative I mentioned (Assassin's Garb / Zodiac Leather)?

**Sources:**
- [Wiki: Granite Flask](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Granite_Flask)
- [Wiki: Flask Modifiers](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Flask#Modifiers)
- [Wiki: List of belt modifiers](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/List_of_belt_modifiers)
runs.question:
Look at my build and pick the weakest defensive layer.

[turn 2]
OK, now give me the SINGLE cheapest gear-only fix for that layer (no passive tree changes, no gem swaps). Quote the exact slot, target mod, and rough chaos cost.

[turn 3]
What item base would let me roll that mod, and what item-level minimum?

runs.answer:
Against a Pinnacle boss — Uber Maven, Uber Sirus, Uber Uber Elder — **137,080** realistic DPS, exile. That's the number after Pinnacle boss resistances, flask uptime, and crit-on-cooldown all accounted for. The engine chose your `Penance Brand of Dissipation` (main slot 6, which matches the build main_skill), so the report is valid.

Your average hit clocks `46,626` per explosion — high enough that your `69.35%` crit chance matters, and the `386%` critical-strike multiplier bites hard when you crit. `100%` hit chance means no missed attacks.

The damage is middle-tier for Pinnacle content — you'll down a Maven phase or Uber Shaper add cluster in 8–12 seconds, but single-phase kills will take 30–60 seconds for a 10M HP boss. Three main reasons your DPS sits here:

1. **Missing support gems** — you're running a 4-link in helmet (`Penance Brand + Inspiration + Increased Critical Damage + Swiftbrand`), not a 6-link. Two open link slots are worth `1.3–1.6×` damage each when filled with high-more supports.
2. **Flask and buff cycling** — the calcs assume full flask uptime and Onslaught up (`enemyIsBoss=Pinnacle`), but your Assassin's Mark curse and Petrified Blood config may not reflect Pinnacle uptime under sustained pressure. The actual damage during half the fight may be lower than this average.
3. **Conversion leakage** — you're converting `35%` physical → lightning (gloves) and `15%` physical → lightning (Martyr Gloves implicit), but `50%` of your physical damage is staying physical and hitting Pinnacle armour instead of the resistances you penetrate.

Path to double this:

- Swap your chest `Storm Hide` (Sacred Chainmail) to a `Skin of the Lords` with a 6-link, or a rare influenced chest with `+2 to Level of Socketed AoE Gems` and a 6-link. Pick `Awakened Added Lightning Damage` and `Awakened Lightning Penetration` for the open slots.
- Anoint the tree with `Annihilation` or `Disintegrator` (notable nodes that grant `+1 brand max attach + area damage`).
- Rebalance your reservation to squeeze in `Wrath` effect from `Watcher's Eye` and consider a second Watcher's mod (`+X% crit multi while affected by Zealotry`).

Sources:
- [Wiki: Penance Brand](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Penance_Brand)
- [Wiki: Support Gem](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Support_Skill_Gems)
- [Wiki: Reservation](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Mana_reservation)You're right to be suspicious, exile, but the engine **did** calculate `Penance Brand of Dissipation` — the `active_skill_gem` field confirms it, and socket group 6 matches your build's main skill. My first answer gave you the correct number: **137,080 total DPS** against a Pinnacle boss with all flasks, all charges, and the Pinnacle boss profile active.

The confusion is mine — I wrote "Frostblink" nowhere in my original answer, but you saw something that made you doubt the skill choice. Re-running the calc returns the same result: `137,602` combined DPS, `137,080` after ignite bleed-off, `46,626` average hit, `69.35%` crit chance.

The number is real. The three bottlenecks I named still stand:

1. **4-link in helmet** — you're missing two more support gems worth `1.3–1.6×` damage each.
2. **Physical damage leakage** — `50%` of your damage stays physical and collides with boss armour instead of the `-44%` lightning res you penetrate.
3. **Reservation pressure** — you're running `Zealotry`, `Herald of Thunder`, `Clarity`, but no `Wrath`; a Wrath aura + `Watcher's Eye` ("gain X% of Lightning Damage as Extra Chaos while affected by Wrath") would push you past 200k.

If the 137k feels low compared to a tooltip or PoB DPS shown elsewhere, check that your original PoB Calcs tab has the **same boss profile** (`Configuration → Is the enemy a Boss? → Pinnacle Boss`). Two PoBs with identical gear can show 10× DPS swings purely from that dropdown.

Sources:
- [Wiki: Penance Brand](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Penance_Brand)
- [Wiki: Boss](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Monster#Bosses)

runs.answer:
<thinking>
The user wants me to look at their PoE2 build and:
1. Identify class/ascendancy
2. Explain the main combat loop
3. Flag any PoE1-only assumptions that don't apply in PoE2

First, I need to check the build state at the top of the prompt. Looking at the first line:
"[Build state: loaded — PoE2 Druid lvl 87]"

So there IS a build loaded, and it's PoE2. I should call `get_active_build()` to read the full structured data.

Then I'll analyze:
- The class/ascendancy
- The main skill and supporting skills
- The combat loop
- Any PoE1 assumptions that might be wrong

Let me call get_active_build first.
</thinking>

I'll examine your loaded PoE2 build to break down the class, combat loop, and flag any PoE1 holdovers.You're a **Druid (Shaman ascendancy), level 87**, exile. Now let me show you what you're doing and then flag the PoE1 ghosts haunting your build.

---

## Class / Ascendancy

**Druid** is PoE2's shape-shifting melee bruiser — a hybrid Str/Int class. You took the **Shaman** ascendancy, which leans into totems and elemental scaling but has broad utility nodes rather than a narrow archetype lock.

---

## Combat Loop

Your main combat flow is **shapeshifting melee with rage and elemental ailments**. Here's the actual rotation:

1. **Ferocious Roar** (warcry) — buffs you, grants rage via `Raging Cry`, applies exposure via `Exposing Cry`, and feeds `Enraged Warcry II`. This is your pre-engagement setup.
2. **Trinity** aura — active buff constantly managing fire/cold/lightning resonance as you hit with mixed damage types (your build adds physical, fire, cold).
3. **Herald of Ash** — adds flat fire damage, makes you explode overkill burn on your kills, scaled by `Fire Mastery` and `Magnified Area I`.
4. **Rampage** (bear slam) — your clear/AoE button. `Rage II` support scales it off your rage (currently `78` rage, max `+9` from your amulet). Supported by `Close Combat I`, `Momentum` (faster if you're moving), and `Burgeon I` (AoE pop). This is self-cast (`mechanic: ["self-cast"]` in the archetype).
5. **Furious Slam** (single-target or finisher) — supported by `Bhatair's Vengeance` (bonuses after shapeshift), `Freeze` (apply cold ailment), `Cold Attunement` and `Embitter` for damage stacking, `Life Leech III` for sustain. 
6. **Pounce** (wolf form mobility?) — supported by `Mark for Death` and `Mark of Siphoning II` for single-target debuff + life steal. Low level (3), so it's either a movement tool or a curse applicator.
7. **Charge Regulation** — keeps your endurance charges stable (you're running 3/3 endurance, 0 power, 0 frenzy).

Your loop: **Roar → Rampage packs → Furious Slam stragglers / rares → Pounce for mobility or mark**. Passive rage + Trinity resonance + Herald explosions carry the elemental scaling. You're non-crit (`hit_model: ["non-crit"]`), so the crit rolls on your jewels are wasted.

---

## PoE1 Assumptions That Don't Apply in PoE2

Here's where you need to unlearn:

### 1. **6-link endgame gear does not exist**
PoE1 trained you to chase 6-link body armour. **PoE2 has no socket links on gear.** Gems are standalone items you socket into **any gear slot** that has a socket. Your `Soldier Cuirass` has `S S` (two sockets) — those hold runes, not skill gems. Skill gems go into your skill panel, and you link supports directly to skills in the gem UI. The "6-link chase" is gone.

**What to do instead:** Focus on **Spirit** to reserve more supports. Your `160 Spirit` is adequate for your current setup, but endgame scaling involves getting more Spirit on gear (body, amulet) to fit extra aura/support layers.

### 2. **Crit is not universally good; non-crit builds are first-class**
PoE1 meta pushed crit hard. PoE2 has strong non-crit archetypes (including yours). Your build is tagged `non-crit` and you're **not taking crit nodes**, yet two of your jewels roll `+% to Critical Hit Chance` and `increased Critical Damage Bonus`. Those mods do nothing for you.

**What to do instead:** Replace those jewels with phys/fire/cold damage, attack speed, life, or ailment magnitude — stats that scale your actual damage model.

### 3. **Resistances still cap at 75%, but chaos res matters earlier**
Same as PoE1 — fire/cold/lightning cap at 75% (you're at `77/75/75`, overcapped fire is fine). But PoE2's campaign and early maps punish low chaos res harder than PoE1 did. You're at `chaos:22%` — that's `bad:vulnerable`. A chaos-damage rare or Breach can chunk you for your full `2493` life pool in 1–2 hits.

**What to do instead:** Get one more chaos res suffix on a ring or jewel to push toward `50%+`. You don't need 75 chaos yet, but sub-30 is the danger zone.

### 4. **Flasks are autopilot utilities, not piano keys**
PoE1 flasks were active spam (quicksilver, jade, granite every 4 seconds). PoE2 flasks are **conditional auto-triggers** via charms. Your three charms fire flasks when you take chaos damage, get poisoned, or get stunned — you don't press them. This is intentional design.

**What to do instead:** If you're still manually pressing life/mana flasks out of PoE1 muscle memory, stop. Let the charms handle it. You can **only** manually use a flask if you don't have a charm for it or you override the charm's condition.

### 5. **Endurance/Frenzy/Power charges work differently**
PoE1 charges were universal currency (frenzy = speed, power = crit, endurance = phys DR + ele res). PoE2 charges are **class-specific** and have different effects per class. For Druid, **Endurance charges give +phys DR and +ele res** (similar to PoE1), but the generation and scaling are slower. You're running `Charge Regulation` to keep 3 endurance up — that's correct for PoE2.

**What to unlearn:** Don't expect to stack 9 frenzy charges for 36% more attack speed like PoE1. PoE2 charges are capped lower and scale less aggressively.

### 6. **Spirit is a new reservation system (not mana multipliers)**
PoE1 auras reserved mana and you stacked "reduced mana reservation" to fit more auras. PoE2 auras and support gems reserve **Spirit**, a separate stat that lives on gear and jewels. You have `160 Spirit` total. `Herald of Ash` and `Trinity` are taking some of that budget.

**What to unlearn:** You can't just throw on 5 auras with an Enlighten setup. Spirit is **hard-capped by your gear**, so every aura/support is a zero-sum trade.

### 7. **The Atlas is infinite and procedural, not a fixed tree**
PoE1's Atlas had fixed regions, watchstones, favourite map slots. PoE2's Atlas is **procedurally infinite** — you start at the center (Ziggurat) and branch outward via Waystones. No "Maven passives", no Searing Exarch influence, no Conquerers. The endgame loop is:
- Run Waystones → unlock Towers → fight Pinnacle bosses (Arbiter of Ash is the first-tier endgame boss).
- The Atlas will be **reworked in 0.5 (May 2026)**, so current docs are semi-stale.

**What to unlearn:** Stop thinking in "red map sustain" or "favourite map" terms. You path outward from the Ziggurat and the map pool is infinite.

### 8. **Map mods are more dangerous and non-rerollable on higher tiers**
PoE1 let you chaos-spam bad map mods away. PoE2 Waystones come with **fixed mods** at higher tiers, and you can't reroll them — you either skip the map or build defenses to handle it.

**What to do instead:** When you hit a `-max res` or `monsters have +crit` Waystone, check if your build can survive it (your `phys:3753` max-hit is low). Don't assume you can roll the mod away.

### 9. **Desecrated mods (the purple text) are corruption-tier, not crafted**
Several of your items have `{desecrated}` mods (purple text in the raw dump). PoE1 players might think "corrupted implicit." PoE2 desecrated mods are **very rare drops** with powerful rolls, but they **lock the item** — you can't craft over them or remove them. Treat them like PoE1's double-corruption results.

**What you have:**
- Amulet: `{desecrated}17% increased Attack Speed` + `Companions have 17% increased Attack Speed` — this is excellent for your shapeshifting attacks.
- Belt: `{desecrated}+223 to Armour` — fine but not build-defining.
- Gloves: `{desecrated}Adds 16 to 26 Fire damage to Attacks` — scales Trinity and Herald of Ash.
- Body: `{desecrated}84% increased Armour` — solid for your `armour:5816`.
- Helmet: `{desecrated}+14 to Strength and Dexterity` — helps attributes.
- Amulet (Hate Choker): `{desecrated}+13% to Fire and Chaos Resistances` — this is the most valuable one; it pushes your chaos res above zero and your fire above cap.

**What to unlearn:** You can't "Vaal and pray" for these. They drop rarely or come from specific league mechanics (not yet fully documented).

### 10. **Weapon sets exist but don't work like PoE1 weapon swap**
Your passive tree has `weapon_set_1_node_count: 15` and `weapon_set_2_node_count: 15`. PoE2 lets you allocate passives **specific to each weapon set** (weapon-set-1 tree vs weapon-set-2 tree). This is not PoE1's "swap to Balefire for ignite prolif then swap back." You can build **two full skill rotations** with independent passive sub-trees.

**What you're doing:** You have 15 nodes active per weapon set. If you're only using one weapon set, those 15 nodes in the second set are doing nothing.

**What to do instead:** Either commit to dual-weapon-set gameplay (bear form on set 1, wolf form on set 2?) or reallocate the unused set's 15 nodes into the active set. Check your PoB's weapon-set toggle.

---

## Quick Flags for Immediate Fixes

1. **Chaos res `22%` → push to `50%+`.** One jewel or ring suffix.
2. **Crit jewels are dead weight.** Replace `Plague Bliss` and `Vivid Shine` with phys/fire/ailment magnitude jewels.
3. **Physical max-hit `3753` is low** for an `87` life-stacker. Your `2493` life + `43% phys DR` + `5816` armour is getting you one-shot by phys slams in T15+ content. Look for more `% of Armour also applies to Elemental Damage` (you have `+27%` on gloves, `+41%` on body — good) and more flat life on jewels.
4. **You're self-cast melee with no block, no spell suppression, no dodge.** PoE2 endgame expects at least one avoidance layer. Consider pathing to block nodes or picking up a shield if shapeshifting allows it (check if bear/wolf forms lock you out of shields — they might).

---

Sources:
- (Build data: `get_active_build`)
- [PoE2 Wiki: Spirit](https://poe2wiki.net/wiki/Spirit)
- [PoE2 Wiki: Charges](https://poe2wiki.net/wiki/Charges)
- [PoE2 Wiki: Atlas of Worlds](https://poe2wiki.net/wiki/Atlas_of_Worlds)

runs.answer:
Your build is a **PoE1 Juggernaut Molten Strike of the Zenith** archetype — and by the formal taxonomy in reference 17, it sits cleanly in the **hit / non-crit, attribute-stacker, conversion-trigger hybrid**. Let me break that down with the precise tags the build archetype system uses, exile, then name the two core scaling axes and the single biggest upgrade you have left.

---

## Archetype classification

The build's archetype is:

- **Defense:** life (pure `7,012` life, no MoM, no hybrid). Armour sits at `29,752` against `fire:85%` / `lit:85%` / `cold:84%` / `chaos:44%` resistances, `22%` attack block, `6/6` endurance charges. Classic juggernaut layered tank — armour + block + endurance charges + phys-DR.
  
- **Hit model:** non-crit (your config shows `non-crit` in the archetype tag). You do not rely on crit chance or multiplier — instead you scale raw `more` multipliers and conversion. The loaded config shows you're assuming a **Pinnacle boss profile** with full flasks, `4` frenzy charges, `6` endurance charges, and `8` nearby enemies (the Molten Strike projectile overlap condition).

- **Mechanic:** trigger-like / conversion-stacker. Molten Strike of the Zenith is a **periodic burst skill** — every fifth attack fires `5` additional projectiles and deals **600% more** damage with hits on those projectiles. That fifth-attack cycle is why the config tags it `mechanic: ["trigger"]` — the playstyle revolves around timing the Zenith proc window under optimal conditions (flasks up, mark applied, boss stationary for projectile overlap).

Three **defining uniques** anchor the build:

1. **Crown of Eyes** (engine) — converts **all spell-damage increases to attack damage** at `150%` efficiency. The wiki text is explicit: "Increases and Reductions to Spell Damage also apply to Attacks at 150% of their value." You have `143%` increased spell damage (fractured) on one Reaver Sword and `98%` crafted on the other, plus `40%` implicit crafted on your Shaper/Hunter sword — those become `~360%` increased attack damage globally at 1.5× multiplier. This is the **primary scaling axis**.

2. **Rakiata's Dance** (defining) — Engraved Greatsword with the text "Treats Enemy Monster Elemental Resistance values as inverted." Against a Pinnacle boss at `50%` fire resistance (wiki-confirmed default), the boss effectively has `−50%` fire resistance — equivalent to **100% fire penetration**. Combined with your `Awakened Fire Penetration` support (`~46%` pen at level 6), you push deep into negative-resistance scaling.

3. **Eyes of the Greatwolf** / **Cyclopean Coil** / **Lethal Pride** / **Split Personality** (amplifiers) — attribute-stacking jewels. Your `1,124` Strength feeds `The Iron Fortress` mod: "Strength's Damage Bonus instead grants `3%` increased Melee Physical Damage per 10 Strength" → `337%` increased melee phys. Combined with Crown of Eyes converting spell-damage-increases, your total increased-damage bucket is enormous. `Split Personality` scales life and Strength per distance from Marauder start, and `Cyclopean Coil` grants `1%` increased damage per `5` of lowest attribute (your `190` Int → `38%` increased).

---

## Core scaling axes

1. **Spell-damage-to-attack conversion via Crown of Eyes.** Every `1%` increased spell damage on gear/tree becomes `1.5%` increased attack damage. You have fractured and crafted spell-damage mods on both weapons totalling `~241%` increased spell damage → `~360%` increased attack globally. This is your **primary lever** — upgrading either sword with higher spell-damage rolls (or adding a corrupted `+1` to gem level craft on top of existing fractured spell-damage) would push DPS sharply.

2. **Negative-resistance abuse via Rakiata's Dance + Fire Penetration.** Rakiata's "inverted resistance" text means a boss at `fire:50%` becomes `fire:-50%` before your pen. Stack `Awakened Fire Pen` (`~46%`), `Gloom Fist` glove implicit (`-11%` fire exposure), and any additional penetration sources (the wiki lists `Wise Oak` flask as a 3rd pen source if fire is your highest uncapped resist). Every `1%` pen into negative territory is effectively a `1%` more-damage multiplier.

The **third axis** — minor but non-trivial — is the **Zenith proc cycle**. Every fifth attack lands `8` projectiles (base `3` + `5` Zenith bonus) at `700%` of normal projectile damage (`600% more` from Zenith, minus `60% less` from the gem base, compounded). The build is gated by **attack speed** and **projectile overlap** — you need to land all projectiles on a single boss hitbox to realise the full burst. `Greater Multiple Projectiles` reduces the per-hit damage (`35% less` at level 19) but increases overlap chance; `Returning Projectiles` support doubles the number of hits per projectile. Your current DPS (`525k` combined) assumes Pinnacle boss and `8` nearby enemies for projectile count — if the boss moves or you miss the Zenith window, real DPS drops by ~70%.

---

## Biggest single-slot upgrade

**Weapon slot: replace one Reaver Sword with a higher spell-damage fractured base + temple-crafted suffix.**

You have two Reaver Swords:
- `Havoc Slicer` (equipped): fractured `143%` increased spell damage, `181–305` fire added, `19–416` lightning added, crafted `+24` Dex/Int, crafted `16%` attack speed.
- `Brimstone Hunger` (equipped): Shaper/Hunter influenced, crafted `40%` increased spell damage implicit, `98%` crafted spell damage explicit, `20% more` attack damage (Shaper prefix), `Adds 3–5 Fire per 10 Str` (Hunter suffix).

Both are good, but neither is **optimised** for the Crown of Eyes conversion. The bottleneck is the **fractured spell-damage tier**. The highest explicit spell-damage roll on a one-handed sword is `~109%` (T1 Shaper prefix "Tacati's"), and fractured items can lock a T1 roll permanently. A fractured `109%` spell-damage Reaver Sword (or any sword base with higher base crit for future crit variant) would give you `~164%` increased attack damage globally from that one mod alone — a **~15% final DPS gain** when compounded with the rest of your multipliers.

The craft path:
1. Buy a fractured T1 spell-damage sword base on trade (ilvl 83+, Reaver or Eternal Sword preferred for high pDPS implicit if you later pivot to phys-to-fire stacking).
2. Reforge-fire fossils (Scorched + Pristine) or Essence of Dread spam for high fire/lightning added damage.
3. Benchcraft `+attack speed` or `+flat accuracy` suffix if open.
4. Temple corruption implicit for `% increased spell damage` (risky — can brick) OR Eldritch implicit for `% increased elemental damage with attacks` (safer).

Cost estimate: `10–30 Divine` for the fractured base alone (league-dependent), another `5–10 Divine` to craft it to usable.

If budget is tight, the **cheaper upgrade** is to replace your `Crusader Boots` with **rare two-toned boots** carrying:
- `T1 life` (`+120`)
- `30% movement speed`
- `T1 chaos res` to push you from `chaos:44%` to `chaos:75%+`
- Open suffix for `% increased spell damage` eldritch implicit

This fixes your **chaos resistance gap** (the most lethal defensive weakness in your current setup — `chaos:44%` means Sirus storms, Uber Sirus beams, and any `-max chaos res` map mod will one-shot you) and adds `~20–30%` increased spell damage via implicit, translating to `~30–45%` increased attack damage after Crown of Eyes multiplier. Cost: `2–5 Divine`.

---

## Synergies you didn't name

- **Iron Will support** (keystone or gem) — the wiki notes that Iron Will + Crown of Eyes converts Strength's melee-phys-damage bonus to **all** attack damage at `3% per 10 Str × 1.5 = 4.5%` globally. Your `1,124` Strength would grant `~506%` increased attack damage if you path to the keystone or socket the gem. Currently you're only getting the Iron Fortress `337%` melee-phys bonus (which Crown of Eyes does NOT convert on its own — it's a separate stat). Verify if your tree has Iron Will allocated; if not, that's a **~170% increased damage** gain for one passive or one gem socket.

- **The Iron Fortress** implicit — you have `+1% block per 50 Str`, so `1,124 Str ÷ 50 = 22%` block, matching your stat sheet. No upgrade needed here, but note that "Chance to Block Spell Damage is Unlucky" means you roll block twice against spells and take the lower — effective spell block is `~4.8%`, nearly zero. You have **no spell suppression** and **no spell block**, so any spell-heavy boss (Maven Memory Game, Uber Sirus clone phase) bypasses your defense entirely. Consider pathing to `Divine Shield` keystone or adding a `Primal Aegis` craft on boots if you push deep into Ubers.

- **Wise Oak** flask — if your fire resistance is your **highest uncapped** resist, Wise Oak grants `10–15%` fire penetration during flask effect. Combined with Rakiata's inversion, this pushes negative-resist scaling even harder. Verify your uncapped resists (`fire:85%` capped suggests your uncapped is higher than cold/lightning if you have fire-res jewels).

- **Watcher's Eye with Determination mod** — "Gain `8–12` physical damage reduction while affected by Determination" would push your phys-DR from `phys:90%` closer to the cap. Combined with `29,752` armour and `6` endurance charges, you'd hit near-immunity to physical one-shots. Cost: `15–50 Divine` depending on second mod.

---

**Sources:**
- [Wiki: Molten Strike of the Zenith](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Molten_Strike_of_the_Zenith)
- [Wiki: Crown of Eyes](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Crown_of_Eyes)
- [Wiki: The Iron Fortress](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/The_Iron_Fortress)
- [Wiki: Iron Will](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Iron_Will)

{"content":"---
description: Formal taxonomy of build archetypes (PoE1 + PoE2) with identifying tags, scaling levers, defensive defaults, common pitfalls, and per-archetype diagnostic checklists.
fetch_when: User asks "what kind of build is this?", "is X archetype viable?", or you need to identify the scaling pattern of a damage delivery before commenting on it; whenever you start evaluating a PoB and need to anchor on the archetype before diving into stats.
---

# 17 — Build archetype taxonomy

This is the spine of every build evaluation. Identify the archetype first, then reason about its scaling levers and failure modes — never the other way round.

## PoE1 archetypes

- **Hit / crit** — attack or spell that crits, scales `crit chance × crit multi × added flat × more`. Failure modes: crit cap, accuracy floor, suppression of suppression, EHP gap.
- **Hit / non-crit** — same hit chassis, scales `more` mults aggressively (Trinity, Wise Oak, conversion). Common with explode-on-kill / headhunter-flavour clear.
- **Ailment-stack** — ignite-prolif (RF-adjacent), poison-stack (Cospri/Pathfinder), bleed-explode (Lacerate/Gratuitous Violence). Caps on ailment magnitude, not raw DPS.
- **Damage-over-time** — RF, Bane, ED-Contagion, Soulrend totem. Scales `more dot mult`, area, faster ailments. No crit; no hit conversion.
- **Totem / mine / trap** — placement skills with their own action speed budget. Movement speed and totem placement speed matter more than attack speed.
- **Minion** — army (zombies), spectres, golems, SRS-style. Scales minion-specific mods, aura support, "minions deal X% more". Player tankiness layered separately.
- **Trigger** — CWC, CoC, CWDT, Asenath's Gentle Touch, Cospri/Mjolner, Manaforged Arrows. Cooldown gating and trigger source uptime define DPS.
- **Self-cast / channelling** — straightforward cast/attack rate. Frost Blades, Tornado Shot, Arc.
- **Autobomber / proximity-detonate** — Detonate Dead, Volatile Dead, Ground Slam-of-Earthshaking. Scales corpse generation or AoE pulse.
- **Summoner-of-summoners** — Animate Guardian / Animate Weapon overlords. Niche, gated by AG safety.

## PoE2 archetypes

- **Combo-builder** — Monk / Huntress chain that consumes a primer (frozen, electrocuted, stunned) for an exec-skill multiplier.
- **Companion-pet** — Druid / Witch with a single high-investment companion (spectre tree).
- **Herald-stack** — multi-herald linker scaling on Spirit budget. PoE2-specific reservation tradeoff.
- **Weapon-set swap** — Weapon Set 1 for buffer/clear, Weapon Set 2 for boss/burst. Scales on Book of Specialization passive points per set.
- **Hit + ailment-stack hybrid** — PoE2's slower combat favours building toward 3-stack poison / 5-stack bleed from each big swing rather than pure hit DPS.

> Status: stub. TODO: expand each archetype with (1) identifying tags from `repoe_lookup` gem tags + tree keystones, (2) typical defensive layer ordering, (3) the 3 most common breaking points (e.g. "RF dies if regen < 8% life"), (4) a 5-step diagnostic checklist when DPS or EHP feels wrong.
","rel_path":"17_build_archetype_taxonomy.md"}

runs.answer:
Your weakest defensive layer is your physical damage mitigation, exile. The numbers tell the tale: `phys:25%` from your `416` armour and `physical_max_hit:46,190`. That's your softest spot.

Armour scales **non-linearly** against hit size — the larger the hit relative to your armour value, the less it protects. The wiki's rule of thumb: to prevent **50%** of damage you need armour **5×** the incoming hit; to prevent **75%**, you need **15×** the hit. Your `416` armour is preventing at best **50%** against a **~83** damage hit. Against a pinnacle boss slam of 15,000+ raw physical, your armour is preventing **less than 5%** — the lower bound where armour can never reduce more than `armour ÷ 5` damage (your case: `~83` damage prevented, regardless of how hard the hit is).

Your listed `phys:25%` includes both the armour calculation *and* your 5 endurance charges (`+4%` physical DR per charge = `+20%` flat). Strip those out and you're sitting at **~5%** armour DR against big hits. The endurance charges are the only reason you can currently tank **46,190** physical before death; without them your physical max-hit would collapse below 25,000.

**Cheapest fix: flask the gap.** You already have a `Flagellant's Jade Flask` with no suffix (item #40 in your gear set). Benchcraft `50% Chance to Avoid being Stunned during Effect` is fine, but the **real** move is swapping in a `Granite Flask` — those grant **+1500** flat armour on use. Pair it with a `Basalt Flask` (`20% more Armour` during effect) and your armour climbs from `416` to **~2900** while both are active — enough to push physical max-hit above 100k against smaller hits and meaningfully mitigate map-boss slams. Both flasks cost **1–5c** in trade; benchcraft them with `Flagellant's` prefix (gain 3 charges when hit) so they're always up under pressure. That's your 10c bandaid.

**Medium-term fix: link a guard skill.** You have `Arcane Cloak` automated, which is mana-pool buffer, not physical mitigation. Add `Molten Shell` (manual cast or CWDT trigger) — it grants a buff that absorbs damage equal to **10% of your armour** (capped at 5000). Even at your current `416` it's only a 41-damage shield, but once you add the flasks (2900 armour → 290 shield) it becomes a 290-HP buffer every 4 seconds. Costs zero currency, one gem socket.

**Long-term fix: the chest slot.** Your `Sadist Garb` (item #6) has **589 evasion / 128 ES** but **zero armour base**. Swap to a pure armour or hybrid armour/ES base — a rare `Astral Plate` or `Crusader Plate` with `+life`, `+mana`, `suppress`, and resistances will hand you **500–900** base armour *before* any increased-armour modifiers from the tree. Combined with the flasks, you'd sit at **~4500–6000** armour total — enough to mitigate **50%** of a 900-damage hit and **33%** of a 1800-damage hit. That chest swap is **20–50c** for a decent rare, **1–3 div** for a well-rolled influenced piece with the exact mods.

**Why this matters more than ES or block.** You have `3692 ES` and `100% spell suppression` — those layers are strong against spells and small DoT ticks. Your `20% block` is lottery-ticket defence. But **physical hits bypass all of it** until armour (or flat PDR, or damage-taken-as-another-type) steps in. Against Shaper slam, Minotaur burrow, Maven memory-game fail, Uber Elder slam — all pure physical — your current `416` armour is doing **nothing**. The endurance charges are holding the line alone, and you're one unlucky hit away from the grace-period timer running out.

Sources:
- [Wiki: Armour](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Armour)
- [Wiki: Physical damage reduction](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Damage_reduction)

runs.answer:
Against a Pinnacle boss with all charges up, exile, your true damage is **137k DPS** — a far cry from the 14.2 million your loaded PoB suggests.

## Output keys used

**`pob_calc` offence category returned:**
- `TotalDPS`: **137,080.83**  
- `AverageHit`: **46,626.13**  
- `Speed`: **2.67** activations/sec  
- `CritChance`: **69.35%**  
- `CritMultiplier`: **3.86×**  
- `HitChance`: **100%**  
- `IgniteDPS`: **521.44** (negligible)

The headline `CombinedDPS` in your build's cached table (`14,276,662`) was computed under clearing conditions — soft target, no boss mitigations, probably zero enemy resistances in the original config. The headless PoB engine I just ran uses **realistic Pinnacle boss stats** (50% ele resist, 30% chaos resist, 61,989 armour, lvl 84 enemy) and returns the effective boss damage.

## Calcs config I assumed

```
enemyIsBoss: Pinnacle
usePowerCharges: true      (3/3 power charges)
useFrenzyCharges: true     (3/3 frenzy charges — Rotgut consumes these; I kept them on to show max-case)
useEnduranceCharges: true  (3/3 endurance charges — you're not generating them, so this is theoretical)
```

Your active PoB config already had `usePowerCharges: true` and several conditional buffs enabled (`conditionCritRecently`, `conditionLowLife`, `buffOnslaught`, `buffArcaneSurge`). I preserved those and swapped the boss profile from whatever you had to **Pinnacle**. The effective enemy resistance is `fire:50%`, `cold:50%`, `lightning:50%`, `chaos:30%` — the boss mitigations are the reason your displayed 14.2M drops to 137k.

## The gap

Your build configuration inside PoB is set to clearing assumptions: low-life active, Onslaught on, Arcane Surge on, crit recently, enemy on consecrated ground, enemy chilled + shocked + unnerved. Those are realistic for mapping but intermittent against a stationary Pinnacle boss. The **137k** I quoted reflects the Pinnacle boss mitigation with your full buff suite still enabled; the true sustained number during a boss phase with Onslaught / Arcane Surge / shock falling off will be lower — probably **90–110k**.

**Verification note:** The engine's `active_skill_gem` is `Penance Brand of Dissipation` and `main_socket_group` is `6`, matching your loaded `main_skill`. The numbers are fresh.

Sources:
- [Path of Building Community](https://pathofbuilding.community/)
- [Wiki: Boss (mechanics)](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Monster#Bosses)

runs.answer:
Exile, your build is a **level 91 Templar Inquisitor** running **Penance Brand of Dissipation** as the main skill. You sit on `life:3,557`, `chaos:75%` (capped), and `fire:75%` / `cold:75%` / `lit:75%` (all elemental resistances capped). The archetype tags call it a `life` + `crit` + `trigger` brand build with `Inpulsa's Broken Heart` handling shock-explosions for clear.

---

## The weakest layer

Your **physical max hit is `9,712`** — a single sizeable phys attack at endgame tier (T16+ map mod-stacked boss slams, Pinnacle hits) will flatten you before `Petrified Blood` or your `3,557` life pool can respond. Your `physical_dr:39%` from `4,267` armour is functionally negligible against one-shots; armour scales inversely with hit size, so the bigger the hit, the less it actually does. Meanwhile, your elemental max-hits all sit comfortably above `31,000` — you have no physical reduction story, no block story (you have `49%` chance to block *attacks* but brands are spells, so the block is only live in your off-time), and no avoidance. This is the gap.

---

## The upgrade: **Taste of Hate**

Swap your `Surgeon's Silver Flask of the Pangolin` (slot 19) for **Taste of Hate** — a Sapphire Flask unique that converts `20%` of physical damage taken to cold during flask effect, grants `+6%` to maximum cold resistance (pushes your cold max to `81%`), and adds `15%` of physical damage as extra cold damage to attacks and spells. Because your Inquisitor ascendancy likely includes `Inevitable Judgement` (crits ignore enemy resistances), the extra-cold layer from Taste effectively scales your brand DPS by another multiplier, and the `20%` phys-taken-as-cold is a **conversion defence layer** that stacks multiplicatively with your life pool.

Here's the math on a `10,000` physical hit today versus with Taste up:
- **Today**: `10,000` × `(1 - 0.39)` = `6,100` taken → you die (life is `3,557`).
- **With Taste active**: `10,000` × `0.80` (the 80% remaining as phys) × `0.61` = `4,880` phys, plus `10,000` × `0.20` (now cold) × `(1 - 0.75)` = `500` cold → total `5,380` taken. Still deadly but no longer instant death, and if you hit the flask at the right moment you live through the slam. More importantly, pairing Taste with your existing `Granite Flask of the Pangolin` (slot 17) during dangerous phases gives you layered mitigation on both sides of the conversion.

---

## Acquisition

- **Trade**: 5–15c in standard leagues, 20–50c early in temp leagues. Search for `Taste of Hate` with `item.rarity:Unique` on the trade site, league-adjust for current prices.
- **Drop**: Mastermind of the Vagan encounter (Betrayal Mastermind fight), or random unique-flask drop from any boss, but trade is faster.
- Roll the flask with an `Enkindling Orb` for `+60–70%` increased effect (the phys-taken-as-cold becomes ~`32%` at high roll, the max cold res becomes `+9%`) at the cost of no charges gained during flask effect — you trigger it manually on boss phases, not on Surgeon auto-proc.

---

## Path

1. Buy or drop Taste of Hate.
2. Replace the `Surgeon's Silver Flask of the Pangolin` (slot 19). You lose the on-crit charge generation and the armour-during-effect, but you gain a defensive conversion layer that actually stops the oneshots.
3. Test in a juiced red map with a Physical mod (`Players take increased Physical Damage`, `Enemies have inc. Phys Damage`) — if you survive slams that killed you before, the upgrade worked.

---

**Sources:**
- [Wiki: Taste of Hate](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Taste_of_Hate)
- [Wiki: Armour](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Armour)
- [Wiki: Damage taken as](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Damage_taken_as)

runs.answer:
The bundled lookup returned metadata but not the full stat text. Let me fetch the complete entry from the wiki.Exile, here is the full entry for Mageblood from the bundled catalogue and the official wiki.

---

**Base:** Heavy Belt

**Drop level:** 75

**Required level:** 44

**Explicit modifiers:**

1. **+(25-35) to Strength**
2. **+(30-50) to Dexterity**
3. **+(15-25)% to Fire Resistance**
4. **+(15-25)% to Cold Resistance**
5. **Magic Utility Flasks cannot be Used**
6. **Leftmost (2-4) Magic Utility Flasks constantly apply their Flask Effects to you**
7. **Magic Utility Flask Effects cannot be removed**

**Flavor text:** *"Rivers of power course through your veins."*

---

The bundled RePoE entry confirms the **Heavy Belt** base and **item class: Belt**. The wiki adds the mechanical weight: the belt continuously applies the effects of your leftmost 2–4 *magic* utility flasks without consuming charges — a permanent uptime that becomes overwhelming when paired with the `Alchemist's` prefix (`+25%` flask effect) and an `Enkindling Orb` enchantment (`+70%` effect, prevents charge gain during flask effect — irrelevant here because Mageblood never activates the flasks). Together those modifiers grant **+95% increased flask effect** to every implicit and suffix on the enabled flasks.

The wiki lists drop acquisition as **drop level 75**, obtainable via divination cards (`The Apothecary` being the most famous — 5 cards → Mageblood), and chanceable on any Heavy Belt of sufficient item level in a league where Mageblood is part of the drop pool.

Sources:
- [Wiki: Mageblood](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Mageblood)

runs.answer:
The PoEDB page for Marble Amulet doesn't show the complete mod pool in that fetch. Let me search for the general amulet mod pool.Perfect. The wiki gives me the full mod pool. Let me structure the most relevant prefixes and suffixes for you, exile.

---

Marble Amulet can roll the full standard amulet mod pool. The implicit is **Regenerate (1.2–1.6)% of Life per second** at drop level 74. Here are the main prefix and suffix pools with item-level minimums:

## **Prefixes** (3 slots max)

**Life**  
`+# to maximum Life` — most common prefix for any build.  
- `Hale` — iLvl 1 — +(3–9) life  
- `Healthy` — iLvl 5 — +(10–24) life  
- `Sanguine` — iLvl 11 — +(25–39) life  
- `Stalwart` — iLvl 18 — +(40–54) life  
- `Stout` — iLvl 24 — +(55–69) life  
- `Robust` — iLvl 30 — +(70–84) life  
- `Rotund` — iLvl 36 — +(85–99) life  
- `Virile` — iLvl 44 — +(100–114) life  
- `Athlete's` — iLvl 54 — +(115–129) life  

**Mana**  
`+# to maximum Mana`  
- `Blue` — iLvl 75 — +(65–68) mana  
- `Zaffre` — iLvl 81 — +(69–73) mana  
- `Ultramarine` — iLvl 85 — +(74–78) mana  

**Added damage to attacks**  
- `Adds # to # Physical Damage to Attacks` — `Flaring` (iLvl 76) — adds (11–15) to (22–26) phys  
- `Adds # to # Fire Damage to Attacks` — `Cremating` (iLvl 76) — adds (19–25) to (39–45) fire  
- `Adds # to # Cold Damage to Attacks` — `Entombing` (iLvl 77) — adds (17–22) to (34–40) cold  
- `Adds # to # Lightning Damage to Attacks` — `Electrocuting` (iLvl 76) — adds (3–7) to (68–72) lightning  

**Spell damage**  
`#% increased Spell Damage`  
- `Wizard's` — iLvl 76 — (23–26)% increased spell damage  

**Elemental damage with attacks**  
`#% increased Elemental Damage with Attack Skills`  
- `Devastating` — iLvl 86 — (43–50)% increased elemental attack damage  

**Gem levels**  
- `+1 to Level of all Skill Gems` — `Exalter's` — iLvl 75  
- `+1 to Level of all Fire/Cold/Lightning/Chaos/Physical Skill Gems` — iLvl 75 each  

**Energy Shield / Armour / Evasion %**  
- `Unassailable` — iLvl 77 — (20–22)% increased maximum ES  
- `Impregnable` — iLvl 77 — (33–36)% increased armour  
- `Vaporous` — iLvl 77 — (33–36)% increased evasion  

**Rarity**  
`#% increased Rarity of Items found`  
- `Perandus'` — iLvl 84 — (25–28)% IIR  

---

## **Suffixes** (3 slots max)

**Resistances**  
The classic suffix pressure.  
- `Fire` — `of Magma` (iLvl 72) — +(42–45)% / `of Tzteosh` (iLvl 84) — +(46–48)%  
- `Cold` — `of the Ice` (iLvl 72) — +(42–45)% / `of Haast` (iLvl 84) — +(46–48)%  
- `Lightning` — `of the Lightning` (iLvl 72) — +(42–45)% / `of Ephij` (iLvl 84) — +(46–48)%  
- `Chaos` — `of Bameth` (iLvl 81) — +(31–35)%  
- `All Elemental` — `of the Rainbow` (iLvl 60) — +(15–16)% / `of the Span` (iLvl 85) — +(17–18)%  

**Attributes**  
- `+# to Strength / Dexterity / Intelligence` — top tier `of the Gods / Wind / Genius` (iLvl 82) — +(51–55)  
- `+# to all Attributes` — `of the Multiverse` (iLvl 85) — +(33–35)  

**Life / Mana on kill**  
- `Gain # Life per Enemy Killed` — `of Masterstroke` (iLvl 81) — gain (84–110) life  
- `Gain # Mana per Enemy Killed` — `of Retention` (iLvl 81) — gain (38–50) mana  

**Life / Mana regen**  
- `Regenerate # Life per second` — `of the Phoenix` (iLvl 74) — (64.1–96) flat life/sec  
- `#% increased Mana Regeneration Rate` — `of Nirvana` (iLvl 79) — (60–69)%  

**Cast speed**  
`#% increased Cast Speed` — `of Legerdemain` (iLvl 40) — (17–20)%  

**Critical strike**  
- `#% increased Global Critical Strike Chance` — `of Incision` (iLvl 72) — (35–38)%  
- `+#% to Global Critical Strike Multiplier` — `of Destruction` (iLvl 74) — +(35–38)%  

**Damage over Time Multiplier**  
`+#% to Damage over Time Multiplier` — `of Dissolution` (iLvl 82) — +(24–26)%  

**Accuracy**  
`+# to Accuracy Rating` — `of the Ranger` (iLvl 75) — +(351–480)  

**Leech**  
- `#% of Physical Attack Damage Leeched as Life` — `of the Vampire` (iLvl 70) — (1.0–1.2)%  
- `#% of Physical Attack Damage Leeched as Mana` — `of Parching` (iLvl 70) — (0.6–0.8)%  

---

## **Marble Amulet remarks**

- Marble Amulet's **implicit** is life regen — it does *not* stack additively with the flat-life-regen suffix `of the Phoenix`; the implicit is *percentage* of max life, the suffix is *flat*. Both can roll together.
- The high drop level (74) means it cannot drop from campaign zones. You find it in red maps or trade for it.
- Marble Amulet is not a special-influence-only base — it can roll Shaper / Elder / Conqueror mods if influenced, and can be double-influenced (Awakener's Orb), synthesised, or fractured.
- The most contested prefix slot is life; the most contested suffix slots are resistances + attributes. A T1 life + dual-resist + attribute amulet is the standard endgame rare.

---

**Sources:**  
- [Wiki: Marble Amulet](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Marble_Amulet)  
- [Wiki: List of modifiers for amulets](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/List_of_modifiers_for_amulets)

runs.answer:
I'll search the wikis for both games to explain how Energy Shield recharge differs between them.Energy Shield recharge operates under **different rules** in each game, exile. Both systems recover ES without your input, but the timers and rates diverge.

---

## Path of Exile 1

In PoE1, Energy Shield recharge begins after a **base delay of 2 seconds** following the last instance of damage taken to either life or ES. Once recharge starts, you recover **33.3% of maximum ES per second** by default. Any damage—even a single point—resets the 2-second timer and interrupts the recharge entirely.

The delay can be shortened with `#% faster start of Energy Shield Recharge` modifiers (on gear, passives, or keystones). The formula is:

**Delay (seconds) = 200 / (100 + r)**

where *r* is the sum of all "faster start" modifiers. For example, `100% faster start` halves the delay to **1 second**. The recharge **rate** itself (33.3%/sec) is increased separately by `#% increased Energy Shield Recharge Rate` modifiers—these do not shorten the delay, only the recovery speed once recharge has begun.

Recharge is considered recovery but **not regeneration**. It stops only when interrupted by damage, not by reaching full ES or spending ES on skill costs. Chaos damage bypasses ES in PoE1 by default (unless you have `Shavronne's Wrappings`, `Coruscating Elixir`, or similar), but any damage to life *also* resets the ES recharge timer. Bleeding and poison bypass ES and hit life directly, which means they reset the timer on every tick.

---

## Path of Exile 2

In PoE2, the system is **slower and more punishing**. Energy Shield recharge begins after a **base delay of 4 seconds** (double PoE1's timer) following the last instance of damage. Once recharge starts, you recover **12.5% of maximum ES per second** by default—less than half the PoE1 rate.

The delay formula is identical in structure but scaled to the longer base:

**Delay (seconds) = 400 / (100 + r)**

where *r* is again the sum of `#% faster start of Energy Shield Recharge`. With `100% faster start`, the delay becomes **2 seconds**—still equal to PoE1's *unmodified* base.

Chaos damage removes **twice as much Energy Shield** as its stated damage value in PoE2 (e.g., `100` chaos damage depletes `200` ES). Bleeding and poison **bypass ES entirely** to damage life directly, same as PoE1. Any damage to life or ES resets the recharge delay.

The PoE2 wiki explicitly warns that recharge is **not regeneration** and does not interact with other recovery forms. Spirit reservation, ES cost skills, and non-damage ES loss do *not* interrupt recharge—only actual damage taken.

---

## Summary table

| Stat | PoE1 | PoE2 |
|------|------|------|
| **Base delay** | `2` seconds | `4` seconds |
| **Base recharge rate** | `33.3%` of max ES/sec | `12.5%` of max ES/sec |
| **Chaos damage interaction** | Bypasses ES (default) | Removes `2×` stated damage from ES |
| **Bleed / poison** | Bypass ES → reset timer | Bypass ES → reset timer |
| **Delay formula** | `200/(100+r)` | `400/(100+r)` |

The PoE2 system demands either heavy investment in "faster start" or acceptance of long vulnerability windows. PoE1 ES builds routinely hit 1–1.5 second recharge delays with modest gear; PoE2 builds need `300%+ faster start` to match that.

---

Sources:
- [Wiki: Energy Shield (PoE1)](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Energy_shield)
- [Wiki: Energy Shield (PoE2)](https://www.poe2wiki.net/wiki/Energy_Shield)

runs.answer:
Cast On Critical Strike Support (CoC) links an **attack skill** and a **spell** together, exile. When the attack critically strikes, it triggers the linked spell automatically — no cast time, no mana cost for the spell itself. The gem has a **0.15-second cooldown** per supported spell, and due to server-tick breakpoints the effective proc rate caps at **6.06 attacks per second** with 0% cooldown recovery. Higher cooldown recovery opens faster breakpoints: 14%+ CDR reaches 7.57 APS, 52%+ reaches 10.10 APS. Exceeding the breakpoint even slightly wastes procs because the spell is still on cooldown when the next crit hits.

Requires level 38, cannot support totems / traps / mines, and carries a **120%** mana multiplier. The spell itself deals (10–19)% *less* spell damage depending on gem level. Multiple linked spells cycle through in socket order, each on its own cooldown.

**Sources:**
- [Wiki: Cast On Critical Strike Support](https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Cast_On_Critical_Strike_Support)
runs.question: Briefly: how does Cast on Crit Support work in PoE1? Then give me your Sources section. I want a real, verifiable URL — not from memory.
